Card Name: TF-001: Create Job_Position__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Job_Position__c custom object with all fields defined in the data model. This object represents both the internal job sheet (fiche de poste) and the published offer, and drives the entire recruitment pipeline.

Checklist item: Object created with correct API name and label (FR: Poste)
All fields created with correct types, lengths, and picklist values
Title__c and Status__c are required fields
Default value of Status__c is "Draft"
Filled_Positions__c defaults to 0
Object has a custom tab with appropriate icon
Record types or page layouts distinguish Draft vs. Open states

Card Name: TF-002: Create Candidate__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Candidate__c custom object representing candidate profiles in the talent pool. Stores parsed CV data and links to a portal User record for self-service access.

Checklist item: Object created with correct API name and label (FR: Candidat)
Email__c has a unique constraint
Journey_Status__c defaults to "Active"
Name field auto-numbers (CAND-{0000})
Object has a custom tab

Card Name: TF-003: Create Job_Application__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Job_Application__c junction object linking Candidate__c to Job_Position__c. Central object tracking the full lifecycle of a candidature from submission through final decision.

Checklist item: Object created with Master-Detail to Candidate__c
Lookup to Job_Position__c (not Master-Detail, as spontaneous applications may have null)
Stage__c defaults to "New"
Application_Date__c auto-populates on insert
Unique compound constraint: one active application per Candidate per Job Position
Name field auto-numbers (APP-{00000})
All score fields default to 0

Card Name: TF-004: Create Interview_Stage__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Interview_Stage__c object representing a configurable step in the interview pipeline for a given job position. Each position can have 1–N stages with their own scoring rules and time windows.

Checklist item: Object created with Master-Detail to Job_Position__c
Stage_Number__c is required and unique within the parent position
Passing_Threshold__c must be <= Max_Score__c (validation rule)
Sum of Stage_Weight__c across all stages + pertinence weight = 100% (documented, enforced in logic)
Stages are ordered by Stage_Number__c

Card Name: TF-005: Create Interview_Stage_Team_Member__c Junction Object
Card Description: Create the junction object linking Interview_Stage__c to User (interviewers). Allows multiple interviewers per stage and an interviewer to be assigned to multiple stages/offers.

Checklist item: Object created with Master-Detail to Interview_Stage__c
Unique constraint: one record per (Interview_Stage, Interviewer) pair
At least one team member must be assigned before a stage can be activated (enforced in Apex)

Card Name: TF-006: Create Availability_Slot__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Availability_Slot__c object for interviewers to declare available time slots. Slots are scoped to a specific interview stage and consumed when a candidate books an interview.

Checklist item: End_DateTime__c must be after Start_DateTime__c (validation rule)
Slots must fall within the stage's Start_Date__c – End_Date__c window (validation rule)
No overlapping slots for the same interviewer (Apex trigger validation)
Is_Booked__c defaults to false

Card Name: TF-007: Create Interview__c Custom Object & Fields
Card Description: Create the Interview__c object tracking individual interview sessions. Links a candidate's application to a stage, an interviewer, and a time slot; stores evaluation data.

Checklist item: Object created with all lookups properly configured
Status__c defaults to "Pending_Scheduling"
Score__c must be <= parent stage's Max_Score__c (cross-object validation — Apex)
Name field auto-numbers (INT-{00000})
Candidate cannot have two interviews at the same DateTime (Apex validation)

Card Name: TF-008: Configure Profiles, Permission Sets & Field-Level Security
Card Description: Create three permission sets (HR Manager, Interviewer, Candidate) aligned with the actor roles. Implement field-level security to enforce strict separation between portal-visible and internal-only data.

Checklist item: Three permission sets created and tested
Candidate cannot see Department, Open_Positions, Max_Interview_Candidates, Interview_Stages_Count, Hiring_Manager on Job_Position__c
Interviewer cannot see candidate Email and Phone
Candidate cannot see AI_Score, Interview_Score, Final_Score, Selection_Status, Waiting_List_Position
OWD (Org-Wide Defaults) set to Private for Job_Application__c and Interview__c
Sharing rules created for HR Manager -> all Position's applications; Interviewer -> assigned interviews only
FLS tested with each permission set assigned to a test user

Card Name: TF-009: Create Job Position Management Apex Service
Card Description: Build the JobPositionService Apex class handling core business logic for job position lifecycle: creation, validation, publishing, and closure.

Checklist item: Position cannot be published if missing: Title, Portal_Description, Application_Deadline, at least one Interview_Stage
Publishing fails if stage weights + pertinence weight != 100%
Publishing sets Status from Draft -> Open
Closure archives all applications in non-terminal states
All methods are bulkified
Unit tests cover positive and negative scenarios (>= 90% coverage)

Card Name: TF-010: Create Application Submission Apex Service
Card Description: Build the ApplicationService Apex class for handling application submissions across all three sources: published offers, spontaneous applications, and internal search (HR-initiated).

Checklist item: Duplicate application throws a user-friendly error
Application after deadline throws error for published offers
Spontaneous applications have null Job_Position__c and Application_Type = "Spontaneous"
Internal Search applications are created with type "Internal_Search"
Confirmation email placeholder is invoked after successful submission
Unit tests cover all three application types and error cases (>= 90% coverage)

Card Name: TF-011: Implement Validation Rules & Triggers for Data Integrity
Card Description: Create validation rules and before-insert/before-update triggers across all custom objects to enforce data integrity constraints that cannot be handled by declarative configuration alone.

Checklist item: All validation rules fire with clear error messages (French)
Triggers are bulkified (handle 200+ records)
Trigger logic extracted to handler classes (trigger -> handler pattern)
All constraints pass unit test coverage >= 90%
Error messages are user-friendly and specific

Card Name: TF-012: Implement CV Parsing Engine (AI Integration)
Card Description: Build the CV parsing service (CVParserService) that automatically extracts structured information from uploaded CV files using an AI callout. Populates parsed fields on Job_Application__c.

Checklist item: Service accepts a Job_Application__c ID and processes the attached CV
All five extraction categories are populated on the application record
Parsed_At__c is set to the current timestamp on success
Parser_Version__c tracks the engine version used
Failed parsing does not block the application — it flags it for manual review
Callout is asynchronous (does not block the user transaction)
Named Credential used for external API keys (no hardcoded secrets)
Unit tests with mock callouts achieve >= 85% coverage

Card Name: TF-013: Implement Relevance Score Calculation
Card Description: Build the RelevanceScoreService scoring engine that computes a relevance score (0–100) for each application based on parsed CV data vs. job requirements, using a weighted scoring model.

Checklist item: Each sub-score (Skills_Match_Score__c, Experience_Score__c, Education_Score__c, Keywords_Score__c) is computed and stored
AI_Score__c = weighted sum of sub-scores, clamped to [0, 100]
AI_Scored_At__c is set on completion
Score calculation is idempotent (re-running produces same result for same input)
Service handles missing parsed data gracefully (partial scoring with warnings)
Unit tests cover: perfect match, partial match, no match, missing data scenarios

Card Name: TF-014: Implement Eliminatory Filters & Human-in-the-Loop Validation
Card Description: Build the automatic filter system that checks each application against hard criteria. The platform never automatically rejects a candidate — it flags them for HR Manager review (HITL principle).

Checklist item: All four filter types are implemented and tested
No application is auto-rejected without Filter_Review_Status__c being set by a User
Filter_Reviewed_By__c and Filter_Reviewed_At__c are populated on review
Filter_Rejection_Reason__c is required when rejecting
Rejected candidates are retained in the database (not deleted) for potential talent pool
HR Manager can reverse a rejection decision at any time
Unit tests cover: all pass, each individual filter fail, HR approval, HR rejection, reversal

Card Name: TF-015: Implement Candidate Ranking & Interview Admission
Card Description: Build the CandidateRankingService that ranks filtered candidates by score and selects the top N for interview admission. N is defined by Job_Position__c.Max_Interview_Candidates__c.

Checklist item: Ranking correctly orders by AI_Score descending
Exactly N candidates are admitted (or fewer if < N eligible)
Waiting list positions are sequential starting from 1
Withdrawal of an admitted candidate triggers auto-promotion
Promoted candidate receives notification (placeholder)
Waiting list candidates receive NO notification until promoted or recruitment closed
Unit tests cover: normal ranking, fewer candidates than N, withdrawal + promotion, empty list

Card Name: TF-016: Build Application Processing Orchestrator
Card Description: Create ApplicationProcessingOrchestrator, a Queueable Apex class that chains the full automatic processing pipeline: CV Parsing -> Score Calculation -> Filter Application -> Ranking update.

Checklist item: New application submission triggers the full pipeline automatically
Each step completes before the next begins
Failure at any step does not block subsequent applications
Processing status is visible to HR Manager (e.g., "Parsing", "Scoring", "Filtering", "Complete")
Retryable on failure
Unit tests cover: full happy path, failure at each step, retry logic

Card Name: TF-017: Build HR Manager Application Review LWC
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the HR Manager to review applications flagged by eliminatory filters. This is the human-in-the-loop UI where the HR Manager validates or rejects flagged candidates.

Checklist item: Component displays all pending review applications
Approval sets Filter_Review_Status__c = 'Approved' and clears the alert
Rejection requires Filter_Rejection_Reason__c to be filled
Filter_Reviewed_By__c is auto-set to the current user
Filter_Reviewed_At__c is auto-set to current timestamp
Bulk approve/reject works for 50+ records without timeout
Component is accessible only to users with TF_HR_Manager permission set
Component refreshes the list after each action

Card Name: TF-018: Build Job Position Creation & Configuration LWC
Card Description: Create a multi-step Lightning Web Component wizard for the HR Manager to create and configure a job position with its interview pipeline, combining the internal job sheet and offer configuration.

Checklist item: All fields from spec §4.1 and §4.2 are present in the wizard
Rich text editor works for Portal Description
Pipeline configuration allows dynamic stage add/remove/reorder
Stage weight validation: total weights sum shown with real-time feedback
Interviewer assignment per stage via User lookup
"Save as Draft" creates position in Draft status
"Publish" validates all rules and sets status to Open
Responsive design for standard Lightning page

Card Name: TF-019: Build Candidate Application Submission LWC (Portal)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the candidate-facing portal to browse open positions and submit applications. Must only display portal-visible fields (Title, Description, Deadline).

Checklist item: Only positions with Status = "Open" and Application_Deadline >= today are shown
Department, Open_Positions, Max_Interview_Candidates are NOT displayed
CV upload supports PDF format (validated)
Duplicate application (same candidate + same position) shows user-friendly error
Spontaneous application flow works without selecting a position
Confirmation email is sent after submission (calls notification service placeholder)
Component is accessible to Community/Experience Cloud guest or authenticated users

Card Name: TF-020: Implement Interviewer Availability Management Service
Card Description: Build the AvailabilityService Apex class for interviewers to manage availability slots for each interview stage. Slots must respect stage time windows and prevent double-booking.

Checklist item: Slots outside the stage window are rejected with clear error
Overlapping slots for the same interviewer are rejected
getAvailableSlots excludes booked slots (Is_Booked__c = true)
Cancelling a booked slot triggers the interviewer unavailability flow (TF-024)
Bulk creation handles 100+ slots in a single transaction
Unit tests >= 90% coverage with edge cases: boundary dates, overlapping times, cancellation

Card Name: TF-021: Implement Candidate Self-Scheduling Service
Card Description: Build the SchedulingService Apex class that powers candidate self-scheduling. Includes cross-offer conflict detection to prevent a candidate from having two simultaneous interviews.

Checklist item: Only unbooked slots within the stage window are returned
Slots conflicting with candidate's other interviews are excluded
Booking is atomic — slot and interview are created in the same transaction
Double-booking the same slot by two candidates is prevented (optimistic locking)
Rescheduling is allowed up to 24h before the scheduled time
Rescheduling releases the old slot and books the new one atomically
Interview__c.Rescheduled_From__c / Rescheduled_To__c are linked on reschedule
Cancellation releases the slot and sets interview status to "Cancelled"
Unit tests cover: booking, conflict detection, reschedule, cancel, race condition

Card Name: TF-022: Implement Scheduling Reminder & Escalation Automation
Card Description: Build time-based automations: 48h reminder if candidate hasn't scheduled, 72h escalation to HR Manager, and 24h lock preventing rescheduling before the interview.

Checklist item: 48h reminder email is sent to candidates who haven't booked
72h escalation creates a Task for the HR Manager with candidate details
Rescheduling is blocked within 24h of the interview (service validates)
Batch handles thousands of records without governor limits
Reminder is sent only once per interview
Escalation is sent only once per interview
Unit tests cover: all time thresholds, boundary conditions, already-booked scenarios

Card Name: TF-023: Implement Interview Pipeline Progression Service
Card Description: Build the PipelineProgressionService managing candidate advancement through interview stages. A candidate progresses only when BOTH conditions are met: score >= threshold AND interviewer validates.

Checklist item: Progression requires BOTH score threshold AND explicit interviewer approval
Missing either condition results in elimination
Next stage interview is created automatically with Status "Pending_Scheduling"
Candidate reaching the last stage enters "Final_Review"
Eliminated candidates' records are preserved for talent pool
Email notification sent for both advancement and elimination
Unit tests cover: advance, eliminate (score fail), eliminate (no approval), last stage completion

Card Name: TF-024: Implement Interviewer Unavailability & Substitution Logic
Card Description: Build the substitution logic for when an interviewer becomes unavailable after a confirmed interview. Platform first attempts automatic substitution, then escalates to HR Manager.

Checklist item: Automatic substitution attempts all other stage team members
If substitution succeeds, candidate is notified with new scheduling link
If substitution fails, HR Manager receives an urgent alert
Original interview is cancelled with Cancellation_Reason__c populated
Auto_Reassigned__c flag correctly set on the new interview
The candidate's slot conflict check still applies for the new booking
Unit tests cover: successful substitution, no substitute available, multiple affected interviews

Card Name: TF-025: Build Interviewer Availability Management LWC
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for interviewers to manage availability slots. Includes a calendar view across all assigned stages, slot creation, and unavailability marking.

Checklist item: Calendar displays slots across all assigned stages
New slots respect stage window boundaries (visual + backend validation)
Overlapping slot creation is prevented with user-friendly error
Booked slots are visually distinct and non-deletable
"Mark unavailable" on a booked slot confirms the action and triggers TF-024
Component is responsive and works in Lightning Experience
Only accessible to users with TF_Interviewer permission set

Card Name: TF-026: Build Candidate Self-Scheduling LWC (Portal)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the candidate-facing portal enabling self-service interview scheduling. Shows available slots, handles booking confirmation, rescheduling, and cancellation.

Checklist item: Only available, non-conflicting slots are shown
Selecting a slot shows a confirmation dialog before booking
After confirmation, slot is immediately locked (other candidates see it as unavailable)
Confirmation email is triggered for both candidate and interviewer
Reschedule option available until 24h before, then disabled
Cancel option available with confirmation dialog
Component works in Experience Cloud (Community)
Handles concurrent booking attempts gracefully (shows error if slot was just taken)

Card Name: TF-027: Build Interview Pipeline Kanban View LWC (HR Manager)
Card Description: Create a Kanban-style Lightning Web Component for the HR Manager to visualize and manage the interview pipeline for a specific job position, with candidates as cards across stage columns.

Checklist item: All stages for the selected position are displayed as columns
Candidates are placed in the correct column based on their current interview stage
Card displays key info: name, score, interview date, status
Clicking a card navigates to the application detail
Column headers show candidate count
Real-time refresh when data changes (Platform Events or polling)
Accessible only to TF_HR_Manager permission set

Card Name: TF-028: Implement AI Interview Preparation Service
Card Description: Build InterviewPrepService to auto-generate an AI-powered preparation dossier for each interviewer when an interview is scheduled, including candidate summary, strengths, gaps, and suggested questions.

Checklist item: Preparation dossier is generated when interview status changes to "Scheduled"
All five dossier sections are populated
Questions account for the stage type (HR questions for HR stage, technical for tech stage)
For stages 2+, previous interview notes/scores are factored into question generation
Dossier is viewable by the assigned interviewer only
AI callout uses Named Credential (no hardcoded keys)
Graceful failure: if AI call fails, interviewer sees "Preparation unavailable" message
Unit tests with mock callouts >= 85% coverage

Card Name: TF-029: Implement AI Real-Time Interview Notes Service
Card Description: Build the optional AI note-taking assistant (InterviewNotesService) that captures key points during an interview session in real-time. Opt-in only — interviewers must activate it.

Checklist item: Note capture is opt-in (not auto-activated)
AI identifies competencies mentioned and behavioral signals
Structured summary is generated at session end
Interviewer can modify the AI summary before final save
AI notes are stored separately from interviewer's manual notes (AI_Notes__c vs Notes__c)
Service handles session interruptions gracefully
Unit tests cover: activation, input processing, summary generation, rejection

Card Name: TF-030: Implement Post-Interview Evaluation Service
Card Description: Build the service handling the interviewer's post-interview evaluation submission: score, recommendation, notes, and explicit advancement decision. Triggers pipeline progression.

Checklist item: Score validation ensures value <= stage's Max_Score
All four evaluation fields must be filled (validation)
AI_Score_Suggestion__c is displayed as a suggestion but not pre-filled in Score__c
Interview status transitions from "In_Progress" or "Scheduled" to "Completed"
Pipeline progression is triggered automatically after evaluation save
Evaluation is editable by the interviewer for 24h after submission, then locked
Unit tests cover: valid submission, score over max, missing fields, auto-progression trigger

Card Name: TF-031: Implement Final Score Calculation Service
Card Description: Build FinalScoreService to calculate the final composite score once a candidate completes all interview stages, combining the initial relevance score and all interview scores with configurable weights.

Checklist item: Score is computed correctly using the weighted formula
Individual interview scores are normalized to 0–100 before weighting
Interview_Score__c stores the weighted sum of interview-only scores
Final_Score__c stores the complete composite score
Calculation fails gracefully if a stage has no completed interview
Unit tests with multiple scenarios: 2-stage, 3-stage, different weight configs

Card Name: TF-032: Implement Final Selection & Status Assignment Service
Card Description: Build SelectionService for the final selection phase. HR Manager triggers finalization; the platform ranks candidates by final score and assigns Selected, Waitlisted, or Not_Retained statuses.

Checklist item: Only HR Manager can trigger finalization
Ranking is strictly by Final_Score descending
Exactly N candidates are selected (or fewer if pool is smaller)
Waiting list is properly ordered with sequential positions
Selected candidates receive acceptance notification
Not-retained candidates are notified with respectful message
Waitlisted candidates are NOT notified
Decision_Date__c is set on all applications
Unit tests: normal selection, fewer candidates than quota, tie-breaking scenario

Card Name: TF-033: Implement Offer Lifecycle Management Service
Card Description: Build the service managing accepted candidates' lifecycle: Sent -> Negotiating -> Signed -> Expired -> Cancelled. Includes automatic reclassification from the waiting list when an offer expires or is cancelled.

Checklist item: All status transitions follow the defined state machine
Invalid transitions are rejected (e.g., Signed -> Negotiating)
Offer includes a configurable signature deadline
Expired offers trigger automatic promotion of next waitlisted candidate
Cancelled offers trigger automatic promotion
Filled_Positions__c increments on each "Signed" transition
When Filled_Positions__c reaches Desired_Hires__c, position auto-closes
Unit tests cover: full lifecycle, expiration + reclassification, cancellation, position closure

Card Name: TF-034: Build Interviewer Evaluation LWC
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for interviewers to submit post-interview evaluations. Includes the AI preparation dossier, score input, AI suggestion display, recommendation, and advancement toggle.

Checklist item: Preparation dossier is displayed before/during the interview
AI score suggestion is shown but does NOT pre-fill the score field
Score field validates against stage's Max_Score in real-time
Warning shown if contradictory signals (low score + advance, high score + no advance)
AI notes are editable before submission
After submission, pipeline progression feedback is immediate (toast notification)
Only the assigned interviewer can access this component for their interviews

Card Name: TF-035: Build Final Selection & Offer Management LWC (HR Manager)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the HR Manager to conduct final selection and manage the offer lifecycle, including ranked candidate list, offer status tracking, reclassification panel, and position fill counter.

Checklist item: Candidates ranked by final score with full score breakdown visible
Finalize Selection requires confirmation and is irreversible
Each status transition has an appropriate action button
Signature deadline is configurable per candidate
Auto-promotion events are logged and visible
Position fill counter updates in real-time
Component is accessible only to TF_HR_Manager
All actions update the UI immediately (optimistic or reactive)

Card Name: TF-036: Build Candidate Application Status Tracker LWC (Portal)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the candidate portal showing real-time status tracking of all applications. Provides transparency without revealing internal scores, filter decisions, or waiting list position.

Checklist item: All applications for the logged-in candidate are displayed
Status labels are in French, clear, and user-friendly
No internal scores, filter data, or waitlist information is exposed
Pipeline progress indicator accurately reflects the current stage
Upcoming interviews are shown with schedule/reschedule options
Component works in Experience Cloud
Responsive design for mobile

Card Name: TF-037: Implement Talent Pool Management Service
Card Description: Build TalentPoolService handling the full vivier lifecycle. At recruitment closure, eligible candidates are enriched and stored. For new positions, the pool is automatically scanned for matching profiles.

Checklist item: Position closure triggers automatic pool feeding for all passing candidates
Pool search returns candidates ranked by match score
Pool is filterable by: skills, experience, date of last interaction, score
HR Manager can invite a pool candidate to a new position
Invited candidate receives notification and can accept/decline
Acceptance creates a new Job_Application linked to the new position with Previous_Application__c
Candidate who declines is not removed from the pool
Spontaneous applications are added to pool immediately
Unit tests cover: pool feeding, search with various filters, invitation flow, accept/decline

Card Name: TF-038: Implement Recruitment Closure Service
Card Description: Build the service handling recruitment closure — triggered automatically when all slots are filled or manually by the HR Manager. Generates a synthesis report and feeds the talent pool.

Checklist item: Auto-closure triggers when filled positions = desired hires
Manual closure is available to HR Manager at any time
All active applications are archived with appropriate notifications
Closure email is sent to all candidates with pending applications
Synthesis report is generated and saved (as ContentDocument or custom object)
Talent pool is fed after closure
Closed position no longer appears on the candidate portal
Unit tests: auto-closure, manual closure, mixed state applications at closure time

Card Name: TF-039: Implement Full Email Notification Service
Card Description: Build the centralized NotificationService handling all 12 automated communications. Uses Salesforce Email Templates in French, Custom Metadata for configuration, and logs all notifications.

Checklist item: All 12 notification types are implemented with French email templates
Each notification uses the correct template and is sent to the correct recipient(s)
Notifications respect the "no notification for waitlisted" rule until promotion or closure
Rejection emails are only sent AFTER human validation (never automatic)
All sent notifications are logged with timestamp, recipient, and template used
Notifications can be disabled per event type via Custom Metadata
Templates use merge fields for personalization (candidate name, position title, dates)
Unit tests cover all 12 event types

Card Name: TF-040: Implement AI Conversational Assistant Service
Card Description: Build ChatAssistantService, the backend for the AI conversational assistant. Answers questions in natural language, contextualised to the user's role (Candidate, HR Manager, Interviewer).

Checklist item: Assistant responds correctly for all three user profiles
Candidate assistant never reveals scores, internal evaluations, or waitlist position
HR Manager can query the talent pool in natural language and get structured results
Interviewer can access their assigned candidate's prep dossier via chat
Session context is maintained across multiple messages
Responses are in French
AI callout uses Named Credential
Unit tests cover: role-based context, data access restrictions, session continuity

Card Name: TF-041: Build AI Chatbot LWC (Multi-Profile)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component implementing the conversational interface for the AI assistant. Embedded in Lightning Experience (HR Manager, Interviewer) and Experience Cloud (Candidate).

Checklist item: Chat panel is accessible on all relevant pages
Quick actions differ by user profile (Candidate vs HR vs Interviewer)
Messages are rendered in a chat thread format
AI responses are formatted with rich text (lists, bold, links)
Record links in responses are clickable and navigate to the correct page
Session context persists during the user's browser session
Component works in both Lightning Experience and Experience Cloud
Loading/typing indicator shown during AI processing

Card Name: TF-042: Build HR Manager Dashboard & KPIs LWC
Card Description: Create the HR Manager dashboard providing a consolidated view of all active recruitments with key performance indicators: recruitment time, selection time, interview-to-offer rate, offer acceptance rate, and AI scoring accuracy.

Checklist item: All five dashboard panels are implemented
All five KPIs are computed and displayed
Dashboard filters by position, department, date range
Pending actions are prominently displayed with counts
Charts are interactive (click to drill down)
Data refreshes on page load (or configurable polling)
Only accessible to TF_HR_Manager permission set
Responsive layout for standard Lightning Home page

Card Name: TF-043: Build Talent Pool Search & Invite LWC (HR Manager)
Card Description: Create a Lightning Web Component for the HR Manager to search the talent pool, view matching profiles, and invite candidates to apply for new positions.

Checklist item: Search returns candidates from the talent pool ordered by relevance
All filters work independently and in combination
Match score reflects the new position's requirements
"Invite to apply" sends notification to candidate and creates a pending application
Bulk invite works for 50+ candidates
Candidate detail shows enrichment data (scores, feedback, skills from previous applications)
Personal data (email, phone) only visible to HR Manager per FLS rules

Card Name: TF-044: Implement Decision Audit Trail & GDPR Compliance
Card Description: Implement comprehensive audit logging (Decision_Audit_Log__c) for all decisions made in the platform and ensure GDPR compliance: data retention policy, anonymization on request, and data export.

Checklist item: All state changes across Application, Interview, and Offer lifecycle are logged
Audit logs are immutable (no edit/delete for non-admin users)
Logs include actor identity, timestamp, and before/after state
Candidate data can be anonymized on request without breaking referential integrity
Data export is available in a machine-readable format
Audit trail is searchable by position, candidate, date range, action type
Unit tests cover: log creation on each state change, anonymization, export

Card Name: TF-045: End-to-End Integration Testing & UAT Preparation
Card Description: Create comprehensive end-to-end test scenarios covering the full recruitment lifecycle. Prepare Test Data Factory and UAT test scripts for 8 E2E scenarios including happy path, filter flows, waitlist promotion, substitution, spontaneous applications, and concurrent bookings.

Checklist item: All 8 E2E scenarios pass with assertions
Test Data Factory generates realistic data for all objects
Overall code coverage across all Apex classes >= 85%
No governor limit violations under bulk operations (200+ records)
UAT test scripts are documented with step-by-step instructions
All E2E tests run in < 5 minutes total
